# @tyler-g/multipart-uploader

> Drop-in multipart S3 file upload for any JavaScript app. Handles chunking, presigned URLs, concurrency, progress tracking, and resume â€” so you don't have to.

## Install

```
npm i @tyler-g/multipart-uploader
```

## What this replaces

Without this package, uploading large files to S3 requires manually orchestrating:
1. `CreateMultipartUpload` to get an upload ID
2. `UploadPart` with presigned URLs for each chunk
3. `CompleteMultipartUpload` with all ETags
4. Error handling, retry logic, progress tracking, and resume state

This package handles all of that with two classes: `MultipartUploader` (client) and `MultipartUploaderBackend` (server).

## Client usage

```typescript
import { MultipartUploader } from '@tyler-g/multipart-uploader/client';

const uploader = new MultipartUploader({
  serverConfig: {
    endpoint: 'https://api.example.com',
  },
});

uploader.on('totalProgress', (progress) => console.log(`${progress}%`));
uploader.on('uploadComplete', (s3Key) => console.log('Uploaded:', s3Key));
uploader.on('uploadFailed', (err, localRecord) => console.error(err));

const s3Key = await uploader.uploadMultipart(file);
```

### Client options

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `serverConfig.endpoint` | `string` | (required) | Backend REST URL |
| `serverConfig.namespace` | `string` | `'multipart'` | Route prefix on the endpoint |
| `serverConfig.headers` | `Record<string, any>` | `{}` | Custom request headers (auth tokens, etc.) |
| `concurrencyLimit` | `number` | `6` | Max concurrent part uploads |
| `partMinSizeBytes` | `number` | `10485760` (10MB) | Minimum part size in bytes |
| `maxNumParts` | `number` | `96` | Maximum number of parts |
| `debugMode` | `boolean` | `false` | Enable verbose console logging |

### Client events

| Event | Payload | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `totalProgress` | `number (0-100)` | Upload progress percentage |
| `uploadComplete` | `string` | S3 filename (key) of completed upload |
| `uploadFailed` | `Error, LocalUploadRecord` | Upload failure with error and resume state |
| `error` | `Error` | General error |
| `uploadStarted` | â€” | Upload began |
| `uploadResumed` | `number, LocalUploadRecord` | Resumed from previous failed upload |
| `uploadCreated` | `string, string` | AWS upload ID and S3 filename assigned |
| `uploadPartStarted` | `number` | Part number about to upload |
| `uploadPartSignedUrl` | `number, string` | Part received its presigned URL |
| `uploadPartSuccess` | `number` | Part successfully uploaded to S3 |

## Server usage (Express example)

```typescript
import express from 'express';
import { MultipartUploaderBackend } from '@tyler-g/multipart-uploader/server';

const app = express();
app.use(express.json());

const uploaderBackend = new MultipartUploaderBackend({
  awsConfig: {
    bucket: 'my-s3-bucket',
    s3Config: { region: 'us-east-1' },
  },
});

// Three routes required:
app.post('/multipart/create', async (req, res) => {
  res.json(await uploaderBackend.createMultipartUpload(req.body));
});

app.post('/multipart/part', async (req, res) => {
  res.json(await uploaderBackend.uploadMultipartPart(req.body));
});

app.post('/multipart/complete', async (req, res) => {
  res.json(await uploaderBackend.completeMultipartUpload(req.body));
});

app.listen(3000);
```

### Server options

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `awsConfig.bucket` | `string` | (required) | S3 bucket name |
| `awsConfig.signedUrlExpiration` | `number` | `180` | Presigned URL expiration in seconds |
| `awsConfig.s3Config` | `S3ClientConfig` | `{ region: 'us-east-1' }` | AWS SDK S3 client config |
| `filenameGeneratorFn` | `() => string` | `null` (uses crypto UUID) | Custom filename generator |
| `debugMode` | `boolean` | `false` | Enable verbose console logging |

### Server methods

- `createMultipartUpload(req)` â†’ `{ uploadId, s3Filename }`
- `uploadMultipartPart(req)` â†’ `{ partNumber, uploadId, s3Filename, signedUrl }`
- `completeMultipartUpload(req)` â†’ `{ s3Filename }`

## Shared types (importable)

```typescript
import type {
  CreateMultipartUploadRequest,
  CreateMultipartUploadResponse,
  UploadMultipartPartRequest,
  UploadMultipartPartResponse,
  CompleteMultipartUploadRequest,
  CompleteMultipartUploadResponse,
  UploadPartResponse,
  NumberPercentageRange,
} from '@tyler-g/multipart-uploader';
```

## AWS permissions required

The IAM credentials need `s3:PutObject` and `s3:GetObject` on the target bucket.

## Key features

- Automatic chunking and part size calculation
- Resumable uploads (saved to localStorage, survives refresh/network loss)
- Concurrent part uploads with configurable throttling
- Real-time progress tracking (0-100%)
- Presigned URL security â€” files go directly from browser to S3
- Supports files up to 5TB
- Full TypeScript types
- Works with any JS frontend framework (React, Vue, Svelte, vanilla)
- Works with any REST backend (Express, Fastify, Hono, Koa, etc.)
